title: Humboltia monophylla
taxonomy: Humboldtia monophylla is a species of plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant is also known as the single-leafed tree or the single-leaved tree. The classification of Humboldtia monophylla is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and then the phylum Magnoliophyta. This plant is a member of the order Sapindales.
uses: Humboldtia monophylla, a plant native to India, has been found to possess medicinal properties. Its roots and leaves are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
